name: Groom with Icelandic pony mare
description: Icelandic ponies need plenty of exercise. That's why the Icelandic mare stays on the pasture all year round. Even extreme weather is no problem for a breed as robust and durable as Icelandic horses. But the groom still regularly checks the ground to make sure that it is safe and that there are no branches or objects that could be tripping hazards. She also regularly checks her horse's hoofs for cracks. However, Icelandic ponies are generally very robust and durable, meaning the groom always has a little free time for a pleasant ride in the countryside after all her work.

name: Horse stall with Lusitano mare
description: The beautiful Lusitano mare feels right at home in the cosy horse stall. She drinks from the water trough to quench her thirst and plucks delicious hay from the rack. When it's time for training, her stall door is opened and she is led out by the halter. Maybe she'll win another rosette again soon?

name: Horse Box with Mare and Foal
description: No wonder the Appaloosa mare and her foal feel so comfortable in the horse box: It's so cozy here and they have everything their hearts desire. Their feed is in the hayrack and soft horse blankets are also ready to be put under the saddle. While mare and foal drink a little water from the trough, the cat keeps them company. She has made herself cozy on the bale of straw. But she's not interested in the carrot, which the horse is allowed to nibble on. Maybe they'll both go out to the paddock to have some fun together.

name: Pura Raza Española Mare
description: The Pura Raza Española Mare from schleich® HORSE CLUB enchants with her long, flowing mane that is typical for this horse breed. Her similarity to the Andalusian horses is no coincidence as the Pura Raza Española, or PRE for short, are a breeding line within the Andalusian breed. They have been recognized as a separate breed by the Spanish breeder's association since 1912. You can therefore say that all PREs are Andalusians but not all Andalusians are PREs.

name: Icelandic Pony Mare
description: On a mist-shrouded island far to the north, a small horse braves a harsh, wintery climate and treacherous terrain. Her thick coat and mane are flecked with snow. She looks to the skies and sees the Northern Lights, dancing like fairies among the stars. Something in those auroras calls to her...a mystery, perhaps? What ancient story is waiting to be told in this land of fire and ice? schleich® HORSE CLUB playsets and figurines introduce the limitless possibilities of storytelling and imaginative play. Featuring dozens of toy horses, riders and accessories, schleich® HORSE CLUB lets your adventure stories run wild. name: Oldenburger Foal
description: You can see from the leg length of our Oldenburg Foal from schleich® HORSE CLUB that it will grow up to be a big horse. The young horse has almost fully grown legs at a young age. This makes it easier to buy shoes. Both this and the calm, Nordic nature mean that the horse breed is particularly suitable for beginner riders. But professionals can also handle big obstacles. From low jumping obstacles like microwaves, to large ones like rain barrels outside in the garden, our horse can master anything from day 1. Maybe at some point it will jump so high that the foal's tail touches the clouds. Have you tried it yet?

name: HORSE CLUB Sarah & Mystery
description: Tucked in a grove between Lakeside Riding Stable and Peppertree Woods, a weathered caravan provides a perfect secret meeting place for…The HORSE CLUB! The sound of laughter and friendship inside means Sarah and her HORSE CLUB friends Sofia, Hannah, and Lisa are surely at play. The Sarah & Mystery horse and rider playset from schleich® includes the fully movable Sarah figurine and her loyal Arabian mare, Mystery, plus a saddle, bridle, and riding helmet. description: The sprawling historic country house in Lakeside has been home to the Kramer family for generations. Hannah spends her days caring for Cayenne and Jupiter, the quarter horse gelding and the Hanoverian stallion. After a long day, the family settles in for a home-cooked meal and stories by the fireplace. Life is a cowgirl's dream at Lakeside! The Lakeside Country House and Stable playset is part of the HORSE CLUB collection from schleich®. Open a world of limitless play with more than 100 fun accessories. The Lakeside Country House and Stable measures an impressive 3+ feet wide and 16" tall, with a depth of 9.45" - spacious enough for small hands yet compact enough to fit comfortably in a child's room or play area. And portable, too! The stable comes equipped with all the essentials for a day in the countryside with the horses: Hay racks and feed troughs; grooming brushes and curry combs; apples and carrots for the horses; and cartloads of other accessories. name: Haflinger Foal
description: The Haflinger foal is prancing spiritedly across the meadow, busy discovering the world. It pricks up its ears attentively. I wonder what's going on over there at the fence? This foal is so curious; it's sure to run over to have a look. Maybe it can cadge a tasty carrot from some visitors? Who could refuse, once it starts gently muzzling with its soft brown nose.

name: Peruvian Paso Foal
description: You can see how temperamental the little foal is right away: trotting across the meadow on its long legs, it's happily exploring the area. No wonder, it is a Peruvian Paso and has fire in its blood! Its ears are perked up and its tail is also raised in the air: time for an adventure! When there are no other foals around to play with, it likes to go and nuzzle the walkers at the fence, who always stop to admire the cute foal.

name: Selle Français Stallion
description: When is it finally his turn to strut his stuff? The elaborate braided hairstyle in his mane sits perfectly. The spirited Selle Français stallion is ready and hard to restrain. Show jumping is what he loves most of all! The proud stallion sets his eyes firmly on the next obstacle and takes off at an elegant trot. Victory is within reach, so nothing can stop him now.

name: Playful foal
description: The Playful Foal from schleich® HORSE CLUB includes a play ball that has been specially developed for horses. It has a grip that the Quarter horse foal can hold onto with its teeth. During play the foal mistakes the cute little hedgehog for the ball, but quickly realizes how spikey it is! Luckily neither is hurt in this funny encounter and the two become friends. After frolicking around, the foal drinks from the nursing bottle and has a warm blanket laid over it so that it doesn't get cold.

name: Selle Français Mare
description: The Selle Français mare is on the look-out for an adventure today. Maybe someone wants to ride out with her? Then she could gallop across meadows and jump over fallen trees. Or even better, she could take part in a show jumping competition! That's her favorite thing of all. With her long, strong hind legs, she easily can overcome even the highest obstacles. No wonder she's already won so many prizes!

name: Selle Français Foal
description: Who wants to play with me? This sweet Selle Français foal has its ears pricked in a good mood and is on the lookout for playmates that are just as curious. Like its parents, it has jumping in its blood. Prancing merrily across the meadow it flicks its short tail jauntily into the air. Watch out while it takes a big leap with its powerful hind legs and jumps over a felled tree. Who wants to join in?

name: Horse Adventures with Car and Trailer
description: “Hey dad, where are we headed on our road trip?” With the Horse Adventures playset from schleich®, the destination is anywhere your imagination can travel – a riding tournament in the mountains, a horseback camping adventure, a trip to grandma’s ranch or a ride on the moon. This equestrian road trip playset is part of the schleich® HORSE CLUB collection. It includes a HORSE CLUB Hannah figurine (and dad, too!), an authentic Holsteiner mare figurine, an SUV and trailer, plus more than a dozen accessories for storytelling rich in imagination and detail. schleich® HORSE CLUB playsets and figurines introduce the limitless possibilities of storytelling and imaginative play. name: Small carriage for the big horse show
description: It's show day! Sarah has spent weeks preparing for the big event! She admires the impeccable grooming of the horses and feels a quiver of anxiety watching the graceful canters of the other riders. What will the judges say about her performance? The Big Horse Show playset from schleich® HORSE CLUB captures the emotion and grandeur of horse tournaments. Novices and seasoned equestrians alike will admire the realistic detail, the brightly colored ribbons, and the decorative elegance of the scene. description: Sofia loves picking flowers in the garden – they’re perfect for weaving into her hair and into Blossom’s mane! You’re so creative, Sofia! The Sofia & Blossom horse and rider playset from schleich® includes the fully movable Sofia figurine and her loyal Andalusian mare, Blossom, plus a saddle, bridle, and riding helmet. name: Silver Dapple Mare
description: The Silver Dapple Mare from schleich® FARM WORLD actually has a dark coat, but the so-called "silver dapple" gene means that it is much lighter and looks silvery. Even the eyelashes of silver dapple horses are light, usually even white. This coloring is common in Rocky Mountain Horses, Scandinavian ponies and American horse breeds such as Morgan, American Saddlebred and Tennessee Walking Horse.

name: Horse Stall Extension
description: After a busy day of riding and jumping, your English Thoroughbred, Moonlight, is ready to rest and unwind. The cozy horse stall is calling! A nibble of hay and a gulp of water and your stallion is feeling muuuuuch better. All that's missing is a cozy blanket and some quiet music.

name: Western Riding Adventures
description: Grab them reins and round up the Black Angus calf...there's chores to do back at the ranch, and your trusty pup is fixin' to lend a hand...er, paw. Yeeeeee-HAW! The Western Riding Adventures playset is great for kids who love cowboys and cowgirls...kids who dream of learning to rope and ride.

name: Lakeside Riding Center
description: Yaaaaaawn! Wake up, sleepy head! While you've been snoozing in the loft, other equestrians at Lakeside Riding Center have been getting time in the saddle. Shimmy down the ladder and hurry to the stalls, where your horse is nibbling hay and waiting patiently. Ooooh, maybe after practicing your saddle gait you could take a trail ride to the Rider Café (playset sold separately) for a slice of fresh-baked apple pie! It's gonna be a fully day at Lakeside!

name: Pick up with horse box
description: Because she loves it so much, Betty the groom gives her Friesian mare Raja another good brush. The horse can sense that something special is happening today. And she's right: today, Raja is being taken to her new stall. After she has been properly tended to, Betty leads her carefully into the spacious horse box. Raja has a calm and peaceful temperament, but it still makes her very excitable. Then Betty gets into the pick-up and they slowly set off from the stables. During the journey, the mare looks around curiously. They will soon be at the new stall - and then, first things first, Raja will be allowed out onto the paddock.

name: Pony Box with Iceland Pony Stallion
description: The spirited Iceland Pony loves to ride out and gallop across the meadows. But even more than that, it loves to come home afterwards and rest in its cozy box. This is its home where it has everything it needs. There are even a few crunchy green apples ready in the trough. Mmmh, delicious! His favorites! I wonder who put them there? By the way, the cozy pony box can be combined with the separately available large stable.

name: Tinker mare
description: In the Tinker are the mighty Shire horse, the powerful Clydesdale, the majestic Friesian and the sure-footed Dales Pony. These were bred by travellers to the UK and Ireland to make for a strong and sturdy pull- and workhorse. At the same time they stayed very much on top of the horses characters. They should be absolutely gentle and reliable. To date, the Tinker is known and loved for these characteristics. It is therefore often used as a therapy horse.

name: Tinker foal
description: In the Tinker are the mighty Shire horse, the powerful Clydesdale, the majestic Friesian and the sure-footed Dales Pony. These were bred by travellers to the UK and Ireland to make for a strong and sturdy pull- and workhorse. At the same time they stayed very much on top of the horses characters. They should be absolutely gentle and reliable. To date, the Tinker is known and loved for these characteristics. It is therefore often used as a therapy horse.

name: Mustang stallion
description: Mustangs are also called "mestengos". This comes from a Spanish word meaning "wild, stray". They were originally domesticated horses that the Spanish brought to America by ship in the 16th century and that ran away once in America. Since they found plenty of fodder on the fertile pastures and had almost no natural predators except pumas, their numbers rapidly increased. Wild Mustangs still roam the countryside to this day. They are led by a brave herd stallion who protects his herd and is usually the father of all the foals.

name: Tennessee Walker gelding
description: If you look at a Tennessee Walker from the side, its legs do not stand straight on the ground, but are slanted. This is especially noticeable on the hind legs. The position of its legs enables it to master certain gaits - flat walk, running walk and canter.

name: Welsh pony stallion
description: Welsh ponies are among the most popular riding ponies in Europe. They are spirited but very good-natured and friendly. Welsh ponies can be small and dainty or big and stocky. For this reason, they are subdivided into several different breeds, such as Welsh mountain ponies and Welsh cobs.

name: Black Forest Stallion
description: The Black Forest Stallion from the schleich® FARM WORLD has a very dynamic pose. It shows how powerful and vigorous the Black Forest cold blood horses are. The animals are also known as "WÃ¤lderpferde" (Woods Horse) because they used to help with heavy forestry work. These days the lively Black Forest cold bloods are mainly popular as recreational horses and are used for riding or to pull carriages and sleighs.

name: Black Forest Mare
description: The Black Forest cold blood is a small but powerful and agile horse. It has an even-tempered and docile character while at the same time being very lively and full of energy. As with this Black Forest Mare from the schleich® FARM WORLD, the mane and tail of this horse breed is always flaxen. It creates an exciting contrast to the dark, chestnut-coloured coat of the horse.

name: Black Forest Foal
description: The Black Forest Foal from the schleich® FARM WORLD has a dark, chestnut-coloured coat and an enchanting blaze. Its mane and tail are still short, but the flaxen hair will later be as luxuriant and long as is typical for the horse breed. Black Forest cold bloods are a very rare horse breed that originate from the Black Forest in southern Germany.

name: Sorraia Mustang Stallion
description: The Sorraia Mustang Stallion from schleich® FARM WORLD has a narrow dorsal stripe on his back. This is a characteristic of these horses, as is the dun coat and the dark snout. The mane and tail show another characteristic of the breed: They are always bi-colored! Sorraia horses are considered the ancestors of Andalusians and Lusitanos. It is to them that these breeds owe their high-set, strong neck.

name: Camarillo Mare
description: Snow-white coat, white hooves and light pink nostrils - the Camarillo mare is a true beauty. No wonder she loves to be shown off at parades. Her immaculate coat is a true rarity. That is because all pure white Camarillos can be traced back to one small family of horses in the USA. The original stallion was called Sultan. What are you going to name this pretty mare here?

name: Rider with Lipizzaner mare
description: The rider is out in the dressage arena with her Lipizzaner mare. They train almost every day, refining the different dressage movements step by step. The beautiful mare is a keen learner, and so they both enjoy honing their skills. There's another show coming up - and this time, they intend to win. The rider loves her mare, and always gives her a carrot or another treat whenever she gets a dressage movement right. If they keep going like this, they can definitely win the show.

name: Belgian Draft Horse
description: Imagine you're riding through snow-covered woods in a horse-drawn carriage. A woolen blanket is tucked around you for warmth. All is quiet except the jingling sleigh bells of the draft horse as snowflakes fall gently on you. The horse pulling your sleigh is the biggest horse you've ever seen, a Belgian Draft Horse. And tucked in your coat pocket is a picture-perfect replica, a toy Belgian horse from schleich®. schleich® farm toys for toddlers and kids introduce the endless possibilities of storytelling and imaginative play. From little donkey foal figurines to big barns filled with horses and cows, schleich® FARM WORLD has all the animal figures and farmer figures and sets you need to let your stories run wild! For even more action and adventures, our farm toys and figures play well with schleich® unicorn toys, dinosaur toys, wild animal figures and toy horses! schleich® playsets are built to last. All our toys meet the highest national and international safety standards.

name: Paddock with entry gate
description: Whenever new horses or ponies arrive at Lakeside, they are brought to the paddock first. They get to spend some time grazing there in peace. Hannah's father regards this as very important since it allows the horses to meet and become accustomed to each other, and perhaps even to make friends.

name: Blanket & halter HORSE CLUB Lisa & Storm
description: Lisa surprises her Hanoverian gelding Storm with an amazing gift: a blanket and halter in her favourite colours! The vivid turquoise and gorgeous lilac look fantastic against his dark coat. Lisa absolutely loves it and gives a big hug to Sofia, who spent hours making the blanket and halter for Storm.

name: Washing Area with HORSE CLUB Emily & Luna
description: Emily checks her horse grooming kit: Bucket? Check. Curry comb? Check. Grooming brush? Check! Everything’s ready to treat Luna to a refreshing bath after a long day of riding. This horse and rider playset from schleich® HORSE CLUB makes a great gift for horse lovers ages 5 years and older. The 13-piece set includes a horse washing station, a Holsteiner mare toy, a movable Emily figurine, and loads of horse care accessories. name: Horse Obstacle Course Accessories
description: Training is fun, but winning at the show jump is even better! With the obstacles from the HORSE CLUB by schleich® you can do both. For optimal training, the height of the obstacles can be changed gradually. This way, you can introduce the horses to jumping gradually, depending on their ability, and improve their technique bit by bit. When they are ready, it's off to the real show. There's even a ditch and a hedge with a water basin in front of it. It's a good thing you trained so well beforehand!

name: Lakeside Riding Center Starter Set
description: Yaaaaaawn! Wake up, sleepy head! While you've been snoozing in the loft, other equestrians at the Lakeside Riding Center have been getting time in the saddle. Shimmy down the ladder and hurry to the stalls, where your horse is snacking on fresh apples and crunchy carrots. Ooooh, maybe after practicing your saddle gait you could take a trail ride to the Rider Café (playset sold separately) for a slice of fresh-baked apple pie! It's gonna be a fully day at Lakeside!

name: HORSE CLUB Hannah's guest horses with Ruby the dog
description: There are two new horses temporarily staying at Lakeside Horse Farm! The Appaloosa mare is visiting with her foal, and Hannah and her dog Ruby are helping them feel welcome. Hannah feeds them a few apples for a snack, then it's off to the paddocks to meet the others. This 20-piece playset from schleich®'s HORSE CLUB collection is authentically detailed for hours of storytelling. A removable blanket and halter help the horses feel right at home, while the fence can be configured in multiple ways for limitless play. The gate opens and closes to let the Appaloosa and her mare in and out of the paddock. Ruby's doghouse comes with lots of accessories - it even has a detachable roof to let the sunshine in. schleich® HORSE CLUB playsets and figurines introduce the limitless possibilities of storytelling and imaginative play. name: Tack Room Extension
description: The tack room from schleich® HORSE CLUB is perfectly equipped: Brushes and currycombs are already waiting so that you can really make your horse's coat shine. It loves a bit of special treatment. The cupboard also has a choice of horse blankets you can put under the saddle. The matching bridle is also hanging on the wall, of course. It's a pretty cozy place to hang out, wouldn't you agree? That's what the dog and cat think, too. They're both really excited and keep each other company during the grooming session. But it's only the horse that gets the apple as a reward!

name: Andalusian stallion
description: Only Andalusians that are listed in the studbook can be designated a Pure Spanish Horse (PRE). In order to be listed, they have to undergo a registration and selection process. The horse is inspected and its gaits and physical build are assessed.

name: English thoroughbred mare
description: All English Thoroughbreds can be traced back to three Arab stallions born between 1680 and 1724. Other breeds are often crossed with English Thoroughbreds to make them more elegant. English Thoroughbreds were particularly influential on the development of warmbloods such as Trakehners, Holsteiners, Quarter horses and Morgan horses.

name: Holsteiner mare
description: Holsteiners have powerful hindquarters that allow them to jump very high. They often win medals at the Olympic Games and other major equestrian events. While the average leisure horse is around 155 cm tall, Holsteiners are significantly bigger: usually 165-175 cm.

name: Holsteiner foal
description: Holsteiners are very talented jumpers. Just a few days after birth, the foals will jump joyfully and energetically over small obstacles. However, jumping training only begins in earnest at the age of two to three. By the age of five to six, the horses' muscles are sufficiently developed for them to carry a rider while jumping too.

name: Holsteiner gelding
description: Holsteiners are very popular sport horses and are one of the oldest German breeds. The monks at Uetersen Monastery, located in what is now Schleswig-Holstein in northern Germany, started breeding them in the 14th century. However, the first recorded mentions of Holsteiners are much older, dating back to the year 1225.

name: Trakehner mare
description: A Trakehener mare can only have a foal with a Trakehner, Arab or Thoroughbred stallion. This rule is intended to preserve the breed's elegant build and spirited character. Trakehners originated in the East Prussian town of Trakehnen, near Kaliningrad in Russia.

name: Frisian mare
description: Frisians were named after their home, the Dutch Friesland, and are one of the oldest horse breeds in Europe. They have a characteristic black coloured coat, a magnificent mane and a pretty tail. Another feature of Frisians is the long hair on their legs. The Frisian mare by schleich® HORSE CLUB has elaborate braiding on her fetlock, which makes her a unique collector's item.

name: Arabian stallion
description: Arabians are often referred to as the most beautiful horse breed in the world. They have a slim, noble physique and often a small, elegant kink in the nose. The Arabian stallion by schleich® HORSE CLUB has a braided tail, which is beautifully displayed with its proud pose. He also has a magnificent dark mane and his coat is an elegant, attractive shade of brown.

name: Arabian mare
description: With its calm and relaxed pose, the Arabian mare by schleich® HORSE CLUB expresses that the spirited horse breed also has a gentle and sensitive side. Her tail is an interesting colour: It is black at the top and white at the bottom. Another highlight of the figurine is the dark mane and the dark front legs, because they serve as an exciting contrast to the light coat.

name: Knabstrupper mare
description: Knabstrupper are from Denmark and are great therapy horses. Even anxious people quickly come to trust them. Is it her funny, dark spots on her white coat or her charming nature? The lovingly painted Knabstrupper mare by schleich® HORSE CLUB with her relaxed and friendly pose definitely invites you to play with her straight away.

name: American Saddlebred mare
description: The American Saddlebred mare by schleich® HORSE CLUB carries her head and long tail very high, as is typical for this horse breed. In the past, American Saddlebreds were used as riding and driving horses by the American settlers. Today they are mainly used as show horses. Some not only master step, trot and gallop but also the slow gait and so-called rack gaits. Both are pleasantly soft, four-beat movements.

name: Pura Raza Española Young Horse
description: The Pura Raza Española Young Horse from schleich® HORSE CLUB is a curious, dark, dappled gray horse with an attentive gaze and pricked up ears. Pura Raza Española move as gracefully as dancers from a young age and are therefore often lovingly and patiently trained as young horses for demanding high-class horsemanship. They learn impressive dressage elements such as pirouettes and canters.

name: German Riding Pony Mare
description: The German Riding Pony Mare from schleich® HORSE CLUB has a beautiful chestnut coat. The braids in her mane are held together with cute, small ties. These are a special eye-catcher at tournaments. German Riding Ponies are very friendly animals that are specially bred for riding by children and youth. They look like little horses and are suitable for both dressage and show jumping.

name: American Saddlebred gelding
description: American Saddlebreds are well-known for keeping their heads and their beautifully curved tail high - like the American Saddlebred gelding by schleich® HORSE CLUB. The horse breed has a balanced, even-tempered nature and is extremely willing to perform. Some American Saddlebreds master two additional gaits, the slow gait and the so-called rack gait.

name: German Riding Pony Gelding
description: The German Riding Pony Gelding from schleich® HORSE CLUB has a beautifully braided mane. Despite his low height, he looks like a full-size horse. That is a special feature of this pony breed. The animals have long, slender legs and an elegant physique. This differentiates them from other ponies that have shorter, more stocky legs and a more thickset body. The German Riding Pony is also known as a miniature warmblood.

name: Hannoverian Gelding, Red Dun
description: The Hanoverian Gelding, Red Dun from schleich® HORSE CLUB has a splendidly shiny dun coat, dark legs and a dark snout. He is ready for the next competition with his carefully braided mane and tail. Dressage or show jumping? Hanoverians are exceptionally talented horses in both disciplines. They are competitive, learn quickly, have an even temperament and move both gracefully and powerfully.

name: Hanoverian mare, black
description: The mane of the Hanoverian Mare, Black from schleich® HORSE CLUB is braided into small standing tournament braids. Her lively pose shows that she is full of energy and can hardly wait to perform with her rider. Hanoverians are amongst the oldest warmblood breeds and are excellent tournament horses. They are easy to train, bold and determined, and have a gentle, kind character.

name: Hannoverian Foal, Red Dun
description: The light coat of the Hanoverian Foal, Red Dun from schleich® HORSE CLUB is exceptionally beautiful. The mane and tail are dark, as is typical for a dun horse. The foal also has dark legs and a cute, dark snout. It's sure to be a beauty when fully grown. The erect and refined posture that it already has shows that it's sure to prove a jumping and dressage talent at tournaments in the future.

name: Icelandic Pony Stallion
description: If you hear "Only one more minute!" then our Icelandic Pony Stallion from schleich® HORSE CLUB doesn't want to sleep any longer. Instead, he wants to spend hours in the bathroom admiring his mane. However, he is not only known for his stylish look, but also for his lively and gentle nature. This is probably because he has no natural enemies in his Iceland homeland. Not even the loudest thunder disturbs his peace. So you can plan exciting rides even when the weather forecast predicts summer thunderstorms. The only thing that the brave Stallion finds unnerving is the strange whistling of the schleich® Tapirs. Do you agree?

name: Oldenburger Stallion
description: First, the good news: You've captured a stolen treasure. Now the bad news: An angry crew of pirates is chasing you. Fortunately, your swift Oldenburger Stallion will carry you away and as you gallop away into legend, fame, and fortune. A great new character to add to your HORSE CLUB cast, the Oldenburger Stallion figurine is beautifully hand-painted featuring Schliech's renowned accuracy and attention to detail. Suitable for all ages, this figurine looks great on a shelf-but you won't want to keep it there. schleich® HORSE CLUB playsets and figurines introduce the limitless possibilities of storytelling and imaginative play. name: Horse Shop
description: Ding-dong! Customers are arriving at the schleich® HORSE CLUB riding store. What special treat will you pick up for your horse? Horse feed, saddles, brushes...this tack store has everything! There's even a friendly dog to help you find what you need. Load up your pack, then it's time to head back to the stables...sorry, you can't escape your stable chores by shopping all day.

name: Criollo Definitivo Foal
description: The herd is welcoming a new member today...ooooh, look! It's an adorable Criollo foal! Do you think she loves to run? What's it like living in Patagonia? Does she like apples? We're so excited to welcome her...we've never met a Criollo foal before! schleich® HORSE CLUB playsets and figurines introduce the limitless possibilities of storytelling and imaginative play. name: Trakehner Foal
description: Determined. Stately. Regal. The distinctive characteristics of a Trakehner make the breed a popular choice among HORSE CLUB collectors. The Trakehner foal figurine display every bit of the poise and dignity that define this graceful breed. schleich® HORSE CLUB playsets and figurines introduce the limitless possibilities of storytelling and imaginative play. name: HORSE CLUB Sarah's baby animal care
description: A Quarter horse foal has been born and Sarah is bottle-feeding it! The mare allows Sarah to approach and to groom the foal with the massage brush. Sarah completely forgets the time while brushing the foal. When she remembers, she rushes immediately to the puppy, who is just a few days old. The puppy is snuggled up in the fleecy blanket in its basket and eagerly awaiting its bottle.

name: HORSE CLUB Sofia's fashion creation
description: Sofia has a truly unique talent: making wonderful horse accessories! With lots of love, creativity and patience, she transforms practical horse accessories such as halters and blankets into one-of-a-kind designer items. She likes making things for Andalusians best of all! Her creations are so popular that they are displayed and sold in the Hazelwood cafe.

name: Rocky Mountain Horse mare horse show
description: At the horse show in Lakeside, the Rocky Mountain Horse mare has all eyes on it with its graceful movements. This horse breed has a natural predisposition for the “Tölt” gait, in which at least one leg always remains on the ground. This lets the rider sit particularly comfortably in the saddle.

name: Horse Transporter Accessories
description: A well-maintained saddle is so important! Not only does it ensure a perfect seat when you're riding. It also keep its shape so that it doesn't hurt the horse's back. The is the same with the bridle. How lucky that there are suitable accessories for exactly that! The horse blanket and saddle find optimal space on the saddle stand. The bridle can also be neatly hung up here and there is even space for the riding hat. Keep everything together, and it's the perfect complement to the separately available horse transporter from schleich® HORSE CLUB.

name: Andalusian mare
description: This beautiful Andalusian mare is the darkest black. She is one of the purebred Menorcans, a special type of Andalusian horse that is usually white. Doesn't she look beautiful, the way she sets down her hooves? Her thick mane is particularly striking. It falls elegantly over her neck when she lowers her head. She is already waiting impatiently! When will she finally be saddled and taken out of the stable to go for a ride? After all, the weather is just perfect!

name: Mustang stallion
description: You can tell right away that this fiery Mustang stallion loves his freedom. Look at him standing firm, snorting his nostrils. What do you think he can smell? It must be the whiff of adventure, because he looks ready to gallop off. If you swing on his back like an American Indian, without a saddle, he'll gladly take you along!

name: Holsteiner mare
description: The Holstein mare's lush black mane and thick tail are not yet braided for the show. During training, her mane is allowed to flutter freely in the wind as she elegantly takes a run-up to the next obstacle. She daintily bows her head and immediately jumps skillfully over the wall. Doesn't she look impressive? With her long legs, she also cuts a fine figure in dressage. She's sure to win another trophy!

name: Camping trip with the caravan
description: When Mr. Kramer and Hannah go on vacation, it goes without saying that the Cocker Spaniel and their Quarter Horse mare come along, too. Luckily they have a large caravan and they can all fit in it. Mr. Kramer attaches the trailer to his SUV, and Hannah packs the saddle, bridle and horse care accessories. That way, she can take the mare out for a ride and explore the area along the way. At night, the mare is cozy under her sunshade in the pasture, while the others make themselves comfortable on the couch inside. What a great way to travel!

name: Horse care set, Andalusian
description: The keeper is outside with the Andalusian on the training course. Today all jumps are going really well. Once the noble horse has completed its training for today, it gets a few treats as a reward and is then groomed and brushed. Andalusians are simply beautiful animals.

name: Horse Box with HORSE CLUB Tori & Princess
description: The Horse Box with HORSE CLUB Tori & Princess from schleich® HORSE CLUB is lovingly designed. The prize ribbons, gold medal and trophy show shared riding achievements that the fully moveable Tori figurine has had with her beautiful Frisian mare, Princess. The walls of the box are decorated with beautiful stickers that can be written on. The roof is removable and can be used as a separate stable. Bridle, reins and saddle are ready for the next training - as is the carrot for a reward. While the mare stands contentedly at the hay rack and trough, Tori's cute chihuahua, Beauty, runs around the box.

name: HORSE CLUB Mia's vaulting riding set
description: The little girl, Mia, can perfectly prepare for her first competition with the help of the vaulting set by schleich® HORSE CLUB. The male riding instructor, Tom, steadies the show horse using the vaulting harness and gently flicks the whip in his gripping hand so that the horse runs evenly in a circle within the fence parts, which can be plugged in as desired. Hannah's sister, Mia, has moving arms to hold on to and accept the big golden trophy. Saddle, bridle and blanket are removable and stored on the overhead storage shelf after training. The brown kitten watches the events with interest, as she snuggles into her cosy little basket.

name: Blanket & halter HORSE CLUB Hannah & Cayenne
description: Hannah has a gorgeous new blanket and halter for her Quarter Horse Cayenne. She is delighted with them, because red and blue are her favourite colours. Cayenne seems happy too. The blanket is wonderfully soft to the touch and fits perfectly on her back. When the two of them turn up at the riding arena looking like this, everyone is going to be absolutely bowled over!

name: HORSE CLUB Mia & Spotty
description: Although Mia is only eight years old, she's not at all afraid of horses. One day, she's determined to ride horses as big as the ones that Hannah, her older sister and role model, rides. Although Mia and her three-year-old Shetland pony mare Spotty look very sweet together, they're far more devious than appearances suggest.

name: HORSE CLUB Sarah's Camping Adventure
description: Where will Sarah's camping adventure take her today...a forest in the mountains? A wind-swept meadow in the valley? Adventure awaits in the great outdoors! The Camping Adventure playset makes a great gift to expand your schleich® HORSE CLUB collection. The playset comes with a toy tent and fun accessories like a sleeping bag, a playful squirrel, apples for Sarah's horse and more. You can even personalize Sarah's tent with the flower stickers that come with it. Combine this playset with our Camping Accessories kit (sold separately) for even more fun and adventures. schleich® HORSE CLUB playsets and figurines introduce the limitless possibilities of storytelling and imaginative play. name: Cowgirl Barrel Racing Fun
description: You enter the barrel racing arena at a full run aboard your Appaloosa, hurtling toward the first barrel. Will you go left or right? Hug the barrel tight as you make your first circle, then race hard to the second and third barrel. The crowd roars as your mare bolts towards the finish line. You dismount, gasping for breath, and wait to hear your time...and then? What happens next in your story?

name: Cowgirl Team Roping Fun
description: Lassoing a Longhorn calf ain't no easy chore! You've been practicing for hours...swinging that lariat above your head, throwing the rope, and always comin' up short. Your arms are tired, but you reckon you'll try one last time. Giddy-up! You grip the catch rope, give a kick of the spurs, and raise the loop above your head...and then? Where the story goes next is up to you.

name: Horse Beauty Accessories
description: Thanks to these horse beauty accessories for the Sofia's Beauties lineup in schleich® HORSE CLUB, everyone in the stable can tell the rider and her horse are a team. The horse blanket and riding vest are made of the same fabric. You'll be ready for your equestrian adventures dressed to the nines - giddy up!

name: Horse Transporter
description: Wow, this horse transporter really has what it takes! Lisa has everything on board to travel to the next tournament with her family and as many as four horses. They have such a great time on their way there. During the journey the horses are safe and sound in their boxes. Once they've arrived, Lisa sets up the transportable paddock with fencing. She even has obstacles with her so that they can practice for the upcoming tournament. Lisa never gets bored of coming up with new exercises. And the parents? They get to relax in the rooftop pool.

name: Arabian Stallion
description: When the black Arabian Stallion stands next to the snow white Arabian Mare (sold separately), the two make a stunning pair. But the beautiful thoroughbred also cuts a great figure on his own. With his long, graceful legs and slender neck, he looks simply breathtaking. But he is also really sporty and loves nothing more than a good horse race. His temperament is a perfect match: He is vivacious and outgoing, but also quite sensitive.

name: Arabian Mare
description: The beautiful Arabian Mare sets her delicate hooves down gracefully and looks over the pasture with an alert gaze. A thick strand of yellowish-white mane falls over her forehead. It looks great! I wonder if she knows how beautiful she is. Only the hooves and the muzzle with the large nostrils are dark. She has a pink patch on the front of her muzzle. The rest of her coat is a brilliant white. Attentively she pricks up her ears. Where has her Arabian Foal (sold separately) gone? She'd better go and have a look.

name: Arabian Foal
description: Something's going on back there! The little Arabian Foal certainly doesn't want to miss it! Although it comes from the most noble breeding stock, the little thoroughbred doesn't have the slightest desire to behave like nobility. On the contrary, the Arabian Foal is extremely curious and playful. That's why it pricks up its little ears attentively and has already trotted off for an adventure. On long legs it gallops across the pasture. It looks so graceful that it is sure to become a successful racehorse one day.

name: Hair Beauty Horses Black
description: There are no bad hair days with the Beauty Horses from schleich®. They all have beautiful shiny manes and thick tails. And the best part: both can be swapped out. There are so many different hair colors to choose from. It's not easy to decide with such a variety. But all hairpieces are perfect for styling and braiding. So we may as well start trying them out right away!

name: Hair Beauty Horses Gray
description: There are no bad hair days with the Beauty Horses from schleich®. They all have beautiful shiny manes and thick tails. And the best part: both can be swapped out. There are so many different hair colors to choose from. It's not easy to decide with such a variety. But all hairpieces are perfect for styling and braiding. So we may as well start trying them out right away!

name: Hair Beauty Horses Choco
description: There are no bad hair days with the Beauty Horses from schleich®. They all have beautiful shiny manes and thick tails. And the best part: both can be swapped out. There are so many different hair colors to choose from. It's not easy to decide with such a variety. But all hairpieces are perfect for styling and braiding. So we may as well start trying them out right away!

name: Hair Beauty Horses Brown-Gold
description: There are no bad hair days with the Beauty Horses from schleich®. They all have beautiful shiny manes and thick tails. And the best part: both can be swapped out. There are so many different hair colors to choose from. It's not easy to decide with such a variety. But all hairpieces are perfect for styling and braiding. So we may as well start trying them out right away!

name: Friesian Foal
description: The cute Friesian Foal curiously pricks up its ears and stretches its long neck. What's it discovered that is so interesting? No doubt it will trot across the pasture on its long legs to take a closer look. The pitch-black Friesian Foal is sure to look just like its father, the handsome Friesian Stallion (sold separately) when it grows up. A great future awaits with a career in horse shows or as a dressage horse. This old horse breed is particularly well suited for that kind of work. Friesians are also known to be reliable and gentle – perfect friends.

name: Wedding Carriage
description: Emily looks adorable in her wedding dress. The groom Toby has also dressed up. Together they climb into the wedding carriage, to which a whole bunch of empty clanking tin cans are attached. Hanna has decorated the Lipizzaner and the carriage to make everything look festive. The horse is wearing gaiters with hearts on and the bridle is also decorated with hearts. The bridal couple make themselves comfortable on heart-shaped cushions in the carriage and toast each other with champagne. What is this? There's a secret hiding compartment under the seat! There are some apples tucked away for later, so the Lipizzaner can be rewarded for its hard work.

name: Horse Box with HORSE CLUB Hannah & Cayenne
description: Cayenne greets his friend Hannah with a joyful whinny when she finally opens the door to the box. The beautiful Quarter Horse gelding is a little impatient today because he wants to go outside and can hardly wait. The two of them have planned a nice long ride in Peppertree Forest. Hannah has already dressed for it. She’s wearing a smart hat and a matching Western-style shirt. Cayenne also sports a pretty bow in his thick tail. Hannah now puts the saddle on the horse and helps him into the bridle. Is everything prepared? Well, then, ready, steady, go!

name: Horse Box with HORSE CLUB Lisa & Storm
description: Exhausted but happy, Lisa and her beautiful Hanoverian gelding trot back to the stables. They have just finished training together. It was great fun for both of them but also a bit exhausting. That’s why Storm, the magnificent gelding, is allowed to rest for now. Lisa gets down from the saddle with a flourish and opens the door to the box. Inside, she takes off her helmet and leads Storm into his home. She skillfully undoes the straps on the saddle and removes the saddle and bridle from her faithful companion. Time to relax!

name: Andalusian mare
description: In the 15th century, monks began to breed the Andalusians in the south of Spain. With its graceful appearance, it enchanted especially nobles and kings. Therefore, only few would guess that the Andalusian has long been used as a cavalry horse. Although it was always known for his courage and strength, it is rather their elegance that distinguishes it. It is a unique dressage horse and even dominates the famous leap into the air, which is otherwise known only from the higher riding school of the Lipizzaner horse.

name: Pinto stallion
description: Pintos have unique, beautiful fur drawings that give the horses more personality. Nevertheless, the Pintos do not form a separate breed. They are a colour breed, which is found in almost all breeds. The coat patterns are different in "Overo" and "Tobiano". Overos have a coloured coat with white piebald. Their head is often completely white. It's a so-called "lantern". Tobianos on the other hand, have a white coat with coloured piebald. Legs and back remain mostly white.

name: Clydesdale gelding
description: Many Clydesdales can be easily identified from their white legs. This hair is also known as "feathering". These horses often have a brown coat, but they can also be black, sorrel or roan. Clydesdales are often used as draught horses. For instance, they may haul tree trunks through terrain that is too difficult for tractors to traverse. They are easy to train and keen learners. But they're also excellent horses for riding. However, because the horses are so big, you need special, extra-large saddles to ride them.

name: Clydesdale mare
description: Shoeing a Clydesdale can be very hard work for a farrier. Their horseshoes are not just extremely large, but also weigh over 2.5 kg. This means they weigh four to five times as much as the shoes of warmbloods or thoroughbreds. Although Clydesdales tend to be stocky and extremely heavy, they can move very elegantly. They are famous for their gait: they lift their beautiful white legs very high and with great force. For this reason, they aren't just used as draught horses, but also for shows and street parades.

name: Clydesdale foal
description: Clydesdales grow more slowly than other horse breeds. The foals aren't fully grown until the age of seven or eight, instead of four or five. They then weigh over 1,000 kg - as much as a small car. They are so strong that they can pull objects that are much heavier than themselves. This also means they need a lot of food and water. Every day, they need 20-25 kg of grain with minerals and vitamins, and around 50-60 kg hay. Needless to say, this makes them thirsty - very thirsty, in fact. Clydesdales drink over 100 litres of water per day.

name: Tennessee Walker mare
description: Tennessee Walker horses have unusually long and flat hooves. They are not fitted with horseshoes and are not trimmed. This favours their typical gaits, flat walk (accelerated pace), running walk (running pace) and canter (soft gallop).

name: Tinker stallion
description: Coloured cobs are powerful horses with a full mane, strong feathering, a short back and large hooves. The typical piebald coat pattern was deliberately bred into the horses around the year 1900. They were first called "Magpies". Cobs are very balanced and meek.

name: Paint horse mare
description: How can you tell apart a Paint horse mare and a pinto mare? It's easy to mix them up, because they both have patched coats. Essentially, the only way to tell for sure is to consult the studbook: while pintos are only a colour breed, Paint horses are an official breed in their own right.

name: Knapstrupper stallion
description: Knapstrupper and Appaloosa stallions look very similar, as they both have spotted coats. However, the two breeds developed independently. The Knapstrupper breed comes from Denmark, while Appaloosas originated in the USA. Knapstruppers are not only popular because of their coat pattern, but are also talented dressage horses.

name: Paint horse gelding
description: Paint horse geldings are especially popular with cowboys because they are more even-tempered than stallions. They also have "cow sense" and are very quick and nimble, especially over short distances, which makes cattle driving a doddle. This makes cattle driving a doddle. Paint horses are versatile, strong-nerved and extremely hardy.

name: Peruvian Paso Stallion
description: Watch how this proud Peruvian Paso stallion is prancing around the yard! With his thick mane and gracefully arched neck, he attracts everybody's attention. The pretty ears are pricked up attentively. Maybe he is already waiting to win all the prizes at the next show? It certainly looks like it! He can show off his special Paso Llano gait, which only Peruvian Pasos can do.

name: Noriker Stallion
description: With white socks on his hind legs, the otherwise pitch-black Noriker stallion looks really friendly. No wonder: These horses are known for their sweet nature. Has he won your heart over yet? Standing firm and compact, the Noriker is not easy to upset when he's in his paddock. And that's la good thing, because he can't afford to lose his nerve when winding up the narrow mountain trails of his home country!

name: Knapstrupper foal
description: Knapstrupper foals are born already with their characteristic black spots. But not all Knapstruppers look the same. Although most have a white coat under black spots, some Knapstruppers have a snowflake pattern, which is the exact reverse: white spots on a dark coat.

name: Paint horse foal
description: Paint horse foals have the breed's beautiful characteristic patches right from birth. They are actually just dappled Quarter Horses. In the past, however, only solid-coloured animals could be registered in the studbook. But Paint horses were so popular that just two decades later they were recognised as a breed in their own right.

name: Criollo Definitivo Mare
description: When you love wide-open spaces and riding outdoors, the Criollo is an ideal companion. She's strong and curious...a horse you can trust while exploring long dusty roads and wind-swept fields. So saddle up your schleich® Criollo mare toy figurine...a lifetime of adventures and memories awaits! schleich® HORSE CLUB playsets and figurines introduce the limitless possibilities of storytelling and imaginative play.
