name: Peruvian Paso Mare
description: What has the pretty Peruvian Paso mare discovered over there? Attentively she pricks up her ears and stretches her neck. Even her nostrils are a bit flared - maybe she has sniffed out a crisp apple? She's about to trot off to have a look. She certainly deserves a treat with her beautifully styled mane. She even has braids in her tail.

name: Haflinger Mare
description: The friendly Haflinger mare trots happily across the meadow. Does she want to go for a ride today? Why else has she dressed up so finely? Doesn't she look pretty with the red bow in her white mane. I wonder if her friends have already discovered the little bow in her tail. She definitely wants to be seen, that's for sure!

name: Percheron Mare
description: The friendly Percheron Mare trots up trustingly. She is always happy to have visitors, especially if they have a crunchy carrot in their pocket. She carefully accepts it with her soft, black muzzle. I wonder what her plans are for today? Because of her build, she finds long gallops rather strenuous, but she is very strong and is good at moving heavy loads. Maybe she can pull a carriage? In any case, she is always helpful and happy to join in on excursions. How can you use her help?

name: Mangalarga Marchador Stallion
description: The Mangalarga Marchador Stallion is ready: Who wants to fetch him from the paddock and take him for a ride? He flares his nostrils and snorts in anticipation. He is ready to go out for an energetic ride. Riders appreciate the fact that he is quite manageable and not too big. Originally, the Mangalarga Marchador comes from Brazil. The name Marchador is derived from the way he walks, a gentle gait called "Marcha."

name: Norwegian Fjord Horse Gelding
description: The solid-built Fjord horse gelding examines his surroundings with an alert gaze. He pricks up his small, pointed ears and listens. Can he hear someone coming across the field? He tilts his head thoughtfully. No, it was probably nothing – just the wind rustled the leaves. It's a pity really, because the sociable gelding with its distinctive stripe on his back and the two-tone mane really likes company. Maybe he'll just trot over to the Fjord Horse mare (sold separately) for a little chat.

name: Cheval de Selle Francais mare
description: The Cheval de Selle Francais mare is an excellent show jumper. That's thanks to her strong hind legs, with which she can overcome any obstacle. But she also simply enjoys showing what she is made of at tournaments. That's why she's always up for a workout. She also likes to look out for fallen trees during rides, to jump over before galloping off. With her strong legs, shiny brown coat and slender neck, this horse impresses the entire world of HORSE CLUB.

name: Andalusier Stallion
description: Attentively he pricks up his pointed ears and swings his thick tail back and forth expectantly. The proud Andalusian stallion waits anxiously. Is his owner perhaps coming back to pick him up for training? Hopefully, because he loves that more than anything. You can tell, because the beautiful, pitch-black stallion is world class in this discipline. A quick learner and patient, he practices certain sequences over and over again until both he and his rider have mastered them perfectly. So, how about another training session?

name: Quarter Horse Gelding
description: The Quarter Horse gelding is curious to see how its appearance is received. With its light gray coat and dark gray mane, it is a beauty in all respects. And to top it all off there are those pretty blue bows in its mane. Delightful! Inquisitively, it trots across the pasture to the other horses and proudly shows off its finery. But the soft muzzle makes it especially adorable. This one is black and has a delicate pink patch between the nostrils. Too cute! With these looks, it's easy to forget what a powerhouse this Quarter Horse gelding is. With its compact growth and strong legs, it can easily pull the heaviest of loads.

name: Lipizzaner Gelding
description: Lipizzaners are sociable horses that are easy to train. They are therefore a favourite breed among horse lovers. With his pretty bow on his tail, he also looks great in any nursery. The wonderful Lipizzaner gelding from the wedding carriage is now available exclusively in the schleich® online shop as a single figurine!

name: Horse Club Paso Peruano Starter Set
description: Who gets to ride out today? The Paso Peruano pair loves to be out and about. The proud Paso Peruano stallion is already prancing around the yard in anticipation. How elegant he looks! No wonder he regularly gets to take part in tournaments. With his blond mane and gracefully curved neck, he catches everyone's eye. He paws a little impatiently: Is he allowed out now? But the chances are better for his partner today, the pretty Paso Peruano mare. She is already groomed and her coat is so shiny. Her mane is also beautifully braided. She keeps a watchful eye on what's going on across the yard. I wonder if it's her turn soon? She is already wearing the saddle and bridle. It can't be long now. What a beautiful day!

name: Horse Box with Dog Kennel
description: Come on, now! The horse is impatiently pawing with its hooves. It finally wants to get rid of the bridle and take off its warm horse blanket. The door of the box is still closed, so it will have to be patient for a while. Luckily, its friend the dog is waiting in the dog kennel next to it, helping time pass more quickly. Finally! Bridle and blanket are gone and the horsebox is open. Come on, off to pasture! Before anyone changes their mind, the dog quickly fetches a stick to play with.

name: Riding Arena Peppertree
description: Finally! Hannah, Sofia and Tom can practice new tricks! Hannah gets her pretty white Oldenburg mare, saddles her up and puts the bridle on. She wants to vault with Tom today. He has already hung a new exercise movement on the board and attached the clipboards with letters to the hoardings. Let's see what dressage movements Hannah and her horse will be rehearsing today. Meanwhile, Sofia takes care of her faithful Haflinger mare. They want to practice jumping today. It's working out quite well with the Cavaletti, but they still need to train a little with the harder obstacles. After the riding session there is a delicious reward: an apple for Sofia and a carrot for the horse. Or maybe two?

name: Andalusian Stallion
description: The Andalusian stallion’s thick mane flutters in the wind. Wow, what crazy weather! A gust of wind sweeps the bangs out of his eyes. It's the perfect day for the proud Spaniard to go for a ride. The graceful white horse is already looking forward to being out and about. As a loyal soul, he particularly enjoys keeping his favorite person company. Who's going to rides out with him? Or should he train with the Andalusian mare in the indoor arena? He’s also an ace at dressage.

name: Paint Horse Foal
description: Where’s the cute Paint Horse foal off to now? You can tell that it has a plan, because it has pricked up its pointed ears and looks ready to go. Full of determination it points its dainty hooves and trots across the pasture. Flaring its nostrils, it sniffs the air. I wonder what it’s up to? Maybe it has caught a whiff of a tasty carrot somewhere? The foal comes closer. Surely someone will give in and let it have a tasty snack, as a reward for being so determined?

name: Pura Raza Española Mare with saddle and bridle
description: The Pura Raza Española mare is already wearing a blanket, saddle and bridle, but she is not going to the show today. Otherwise, her thick mane would surely be braided into an elaborate hairstyle. Instead, she is ready for dressage training. She is already looking forward to it and paws at the ground with her slender but strong legs. She is very docile and likes to be led by her favorite human. She really wants to please and has fun too. Who is going to practice with her?

name: Pony Box with Mustang Mare
description: The pretty, cream-colored mustang mare with the dark mane is standing in the bright red pony box. The box is really cozy. The floor is covered with fresh straw and there are even a few crunchy apples to munch on in the metal trough. There is also a bowl of water on the wall. The mare really lacks nothing. All she needs is a name. There's space on the sign outside the box. Who's got an idea?

name: Horse Club Birthday Party Playset with Lisa
description: How old is the little puppy now? Nothing is left to chance at a birthday party with Lisa. After all, she wants her cute roommate to realize how much she loves it. That’s why she got him everything it likes best: a tasty dog bone to gnaw on and a big water ball. They are going to romp around with it straight away. But first Lisa gives the puppy her present. I wonder what’s inside the box?

name: Horse Club Camping Adventure with Kim
description: The little Maltipoo puppy is so excited. It’s on its way to a camping adventure with Kim. She has thought of everything: The tent for the night is already pitched. There’s a cozy sleeping bag for her to curl up in and a comfy dog basket for the cute puppy. But what is Kim planning to do with the big flashlight? Surely, she isn’t planning a midnight walk? Will the Maltipoo puppy be brave enough to keep her company?

name: Horse Club Picnic Playset with Lisa
description: A day out in nature is simply the best! The Rhodesian ridgeback is going for a picnic with Lisa in Peppertree woods. It smells so fragrant here, and the Ridgeback wants to explore. But when Lisa lures it back with the dog bone, the big, brown Rhodesian ridgeback comes running straight away. It’s not every day, that it gets a treat like that! Hang on, what’s that? A squirrel actually wants to steal a piece of the juicy apple pie! No way! One loud “woof!” and the little animal disappears back into the forest. Lisa has to laugh. What a day!

name: Horse Club Sleepover Playset with Hannah
description: The rough-haired dachshund wags its tail happily. Today there is an adventure being planned: a sleepover with Hannah! The girl has already made herself comfortable in the hay, taken off her cowboy hat and rolled out her sleeping bag. She has even brought the dog basket for her four-legged friend. And it wouldn’t be a good sleepover if the cuddly toy horse weren’t here too! It’s is already dark. What’s that crackling sound? The rough-haired dachshund growls. Hannah is soon wide awake. She quickly takes the flashlight out of her rucksack and shines it around the stable. Nothing there. It was probably just the wind making the beams creak. How exciting!

name: Friends & Family BBQ with Sofia
description: The BBQ adventure with Sofia is just the kind of thing the Shetland sheepdog loves. Sofia takes a seat on a tree stump and the two of them make themselves comfortable around the campfire for a while. When the fire is burning nicely, Sofia rummages in her rucksack. What can it be? A barbecue stick and sausages! The dog’s mouth is watering. Hopefully she’s going to share! He sits up on his hind legs in anticipation and watches her every move.

name: Horse Club Day Trip to the Lake
description: On a hot day like today, a trip to the lake is just the thing. Hannah and her pug think so too. The two of them jump into the cool water and splash around happily. Hannah has also remembered to bring the water polo. They love playing with it. Back on land, they make themselves comfortable in the shade under the parasol and get some rest. Hannah prefers to keep the cheeky pug on a lead, because he likes to escape from time to time.

name: Friends & Family BBQ with Tom
description: What a delicious smell! The Shetland sheepdog’s mouth is watering. At the barbecue with Tom, it never leaves the riding instructor’s side. How it would love to have that sausage on top of the plate! But Tom doesn’t seem to want to share. He throws him a dog bone. Well, it can wait! Maybe a sausage will fall off the grill! That will taste even better than one from a plate. As for the corn on the cob – Tom can keep that for himself.

name: Horse Grooming Playset with Appaloosa Foal
description: The foal's grooming care kit is already prepared. To ensure that the little Appaloosa foal keeps still while being groomed, Sofia first puts the stable halter on him. Then she’s ready to get started. She takes a currycomb and grooming brush out of the grooming box and gives the foal's pretty black coat a thorough brushing. Meanwhile, the snow-white Chihuahua puppy looks on in awe. The foal quite likes to have an audience. But most of all, it’s looking forward to the bottle it gets as a reward.

name: Pura Raza Española Stallion with saddle and bridle
description: When the fiery Pura Raza Española stallion appears, all eyes are immediately on the magnificent black stallion. He is already saddled and wearing his bridle. I’m sure he would love to start dressage training right away. He is particularly good at that. He is so dainty and light-footed as he elegantly bows his neck. He’s already trotting off. But wait! Isn't someone missing? Who’s going to train with him?

name: Mucking & Grooming Playset
description: Emily is on stable duty today. Fortunately, she finds everything she needs in the mucking out and grooming set. She grabs the wheelbarrow and sets off for the stables. First she removes the old straw, then she cleans the stable with lots of water. To do this, she has to bring bucket after bucket into the barn. What a drag! Who will help her push the wheelbarrow? She uses the pitchfork to distribute the fresh hay for the animals. The cat keeps her company and watches closely. A little more water in the dog bowl, and then Emily is done. She deserves a medal!

name: Criollo Definitivo Beauty Set with Kim
description: Wellness is the order of the day today! The Beauty Set with Kim is all prepared and the cute Criollo Definitivo foal is already looking forward to the feel-good treatment. Kim massages the little one’s back with the finger brush. That feels so good! Kim uses a grooming brush to make the brown coat shine beautifully. The little foal is only too happy to put up with this treatment. It particularly likes the cuddly blanket that Kim pulls out of the box. It has a pretty pattern and is the same color as Kim’s vest. So you can see straight away that the two belong together.

name: Marwari Foal
description: The cute Marwari foal pricks up its ears, which are curved inwards. Is someone coming? It lifts its pretty head expectantly and looks over to the fence. It’s always up for a game and happy to have company. The foal has the most fun when it is allowed to jump across the pasture with its long, slender legs. It likes to take a few leaps for joy. It looks simply enchanting. Who wants to play?

name: Friesian Stallion
description: The long hair of its mane flows luxuriantly over its back as it canters along. The proud Friesian Stallion is black as night and, with his stately size, he draws admiring looks wherever he goes. He looks especially proud out in the pasture with his cute Friesian Foal (sold separately). This big, strong horse is gentleness personified and very reliable. The Friesian Stallion prefers to stay close to people he can trust.

name: Andalusian foal
description: Andalusian foals aren't fully grown until the age of six or seven. This is significantly later than foals from other horse breeds. In the first few years, they are gently introduced to wearing a halter and being showered. This helps the foals to learn to trust people.

name: Open Stable Little Horseshoe
description: Sarah enjoys her time in Lakeside’s Open Stable “Little Horseshoe”. The horses are particularly happy here because they are outside so much. When Sarah opens the gate to the paddock, the friendly Haflinger mare comes straight towards her. Sarah gives her a carrot and then puts the saddle on her. She would love to go for a ride today, but the foal is still a bit too young. Later Sarah will put the lead rope on her and get her used to it gradually. Just make sure the gate is locked! Otherwise the little one might bolt if it is startled.

name: Mobile vet with trailer
description: Oh dear, the Tennessee Walker foal has been injured. It’s limping and needs to be examined urgently. It’s so lucky that the mobile vet with her trailer is not far away. Mrs. Kramer arrives soon in her pick-up truck. The first thing she does is to take an X-ray. Luckily, nothing is broken. She puts some ointment on the leg and then carefully applies an adhesive bandage. As a reward, she holds out a carrot to the foal. There you go, at least it’s eating! The little one will soon be completely healthy again.

name: Horse Club Lisa & Storm
description: Lisa and Storm are great friends. But they are much more than that: Together they are also very successful at tournaments and have already won lots of prizes. To keep it that way, the two train diligently in Peppertree’s indoor arena. Today is no exception. The black Hanoverian gelding trots a few laps around the arena to warm up. The real action is about to start!

name: Horse Club Hannah & Cayenne
description: Who’s galloping up to us right now? It's Hannah and Cayenne! Hannah is at least as spirited as her bay Quarter Horse gelding. Both love to dash across the meadows at a fast gallop. As a precaution, Hannah has tied her hair into a thick braid and pushed her cool cowboy hat low over her face. It would be a shame if she lost her trademark on the way!

name: Horse Club Sarah & Mystery
description: Sarah and Mystery are delighted. They have planned an excursion for today. Sarah wants to take her Arabian mare for a ride in the Peppertree woods. She has already put the saddle and bridle on her enthusiastic mare. She has also remembered to bring the green saddle cloth. After all, she wants the ride to be a complete success and she doesn’t want the saddle to chafe. Ready? Well then, let's get going!

name: Paint Horse Gelding
description: The piebald Paint Horse gelding is in the mood for adventure today and wants to have some fun! He gallops across the pasture with such temperament that his black mane and lush tail flutter in the breeze. Who will do him a favor and ride out with him? Western riding is particularly fun with him. But he also likes other styles. As long as he is in good company he’s up for anything.

name: Paso Fino stallion - LOVE edition
description: The Paso Fino Stallion from the exclusive LOVE Edition from schleich® is a unique collector’s item that is only available in the online shop in limited quantities for a limited time. This adorable figurine stands out thanks to its meticulous paint and the two hearts on the side. Flowers are braided into the pink/gray mane and the luxuriant tail features a pretty braided style with purple ribbons. This elegant stallion is the perfect gift for all those who want to treat their loved ones. Perfect for Mother’s Day or Valentine’s Day, this charming item is sure to bring lots of joy and is a loving message to those close to your heart.

name: Horse Club Sofia & Blossom
description: Sofia and Blossom are once again the stars of the Peppertree indoor arena. The snow-white Arabian mare with her graceful gait is already a beauty. But Sofia takes great pleasure in making her horse even prettier. She is always coming up with new hairstyles. Today, Blossom has pink flowers in her lush, white mane. To keep their hairstyles in place, the two of them prefer to keep a slower pace today.

name: Marwari Stallion
description: The beautiful Marwari stallion gracefully bows his slender neck and flares his soft nostrils. He pricks up his black, sickle-shaped ears and listens: Is that a whinny he can hear back there? Maybe it's the Marwari mare calling him. He’ll probably trot over there straight away. The little Marwari foal is also close by. With the three of them together, the horse family is complete. Perfect!

name: Paint Horse Mare
description: The patient Paint Horse mare is just like a real family member. She loves to spend time with her little Paint Horse foal or with her favorite people. Because she is so friendly and even-tempered, children are particularly fond of her. She takes her first attempts at riding with them or plays cattle herding in Western riding style. Even now she looks up for an adventure. Anyone want to be the bull?

name: Pura Raza Española Stallion
description: The Pura Raza Española Stallion from schleich® HORSE CLUB is a proud black horse with an elegant arching neck, a noble head shape, a long, flowing mane and a luxuriant tail. Horses of this breed are often mistaken for Andalusians, and vice versa. This comes as no surprise because they are a separate breeding line within the Andalusians. The stud books are kept according to fixed rules to differentiate them from other horses.

name: Marwari Mare
description: The beautiful Marwari Mare watches attentively, as her little Marwari foal plays in the pasture. As she does so, she pricks up her crescent-shaped ears. With a little imagination, from a distance, it looks as though she is wearing a crown. The Marwari mare is curious and doesn't want to miss out on anything. Lucky that she can hear so well with her extraordinary ears!

name: Andalusian Mare
description: The pretty Andalusian mare is used to regular training. She is simply unbeatable when it comes to the art of dressage. Even now, she is eagerly pawing the sand with her front hoof. She probably wants to go to the riding hall for a training session. She pricks up her pointed ears and tilts her head playfully. She bows her neck elegantly. A few thick bangs fall over her eyes. Who can she persuade to practise with her?

name: Paint horse mare
description: How can you tell apart a Paint horse mare and a pinto mare? It’s easy to mix them up, because they both have patched coats. Essentially, the only way to tell for sure is to consult the studbook: while pintos are only a colour breed, Paint horses are an official breed in their own right.

name: Frosty Christmas Horse
description: Holiday Horse #1 is available for a limited time and in strictly limited numbers exclusively in the online shop. This figurine marks the start of a new collectible series that fans won’t want to miss! The coat of the elegant horse figurine features an exclusive special paintwork in snow white with soft blue accents. Tail, mane, and hooves shimmer in silver. The coat and slender forehead are also adorned with intricate ice crystal decorations. A truly magical sight. The dense, flowing mane is particularly impressive. An absolute must-have!

name: Sarah's First Aid Kit
description: Sarah loves animals and would definitely like to become a vet. That’s why she has purchased a first-aid kit. It contains everything she needs to treat minor injuries quickly: a syringe, a stethoscope and, of course, a bandage. She lovingly looks after her two youngest pets, the little Marwari Foal and the Paint Horse Foal. She examines both of them carefully. The Marwari Foal appears to have pain in its leg. As a precaution, she puts on a bandage. Because it stands still so nicely, it gets a carrot as a reward. Sarah does it really well!

name: Lakeside Truck and Trailer
description: The great Lakeside Truck with its large trailer is back on the road. Hannah and Mr. Kramer can't wait: They are heading to a Western adventure with the Quarter Horse Stallion and the Paint Horse Gelding. In the morning, they loaded the great Dually Truck with its wide twin tires with saddles, bridle, curry comb, grooming brush, bucket and water hose so that they could clean their two horses before the event. They packed the cowboy hats for themselves. They will take a break along the way. How practical that the roof of the trailer can also be used as a shelter. Hannah has also packed a few barrels with bars. She has scheduled a training session before the Western adventure. The labrador retriever is also on board. There is plenty of space for them in the trailer. It can transport up to five horses! Yee-haw – Western adventures here we come!

name: Modular horsebox
description: The modular horse box from schleich is the perfect home for all your favorite schleich horses. You get to choose which horse goes into the cozy box. The stickers can be colored in and the information sheet on the back of the packaging can be filled in to match your favorite horse. The horse box can also be combined and equipped with other articles from schleich HORSE CLUB and is the perfect addition to the world of HORSE CLUB.

name: Riding centre with rider and horses
description: Finally! The new stable building is ready! Excitedly, Lissi gets into the car. Her mum sets off. Hopefully it really is as beautiful as Mr Miller, the owner, promised! hopes Lissi. She leaps out the instant they arrive at the stables. Wow! It's even more beautiful than I imagined! With its green doors and pink windows and window frames, the stable building really does look simply incredible. Lissi immediately runs over from the car to the stable building. Her beloved Arab mare, Stella, is in one of the big stalls. "I missed you so much! she exclaims. The two days they'd had to wait before they could finally see each other again had really felt like ages. She embraces her horse and fetches the feed box with apples and carrots. She feeds Stella - and then the two of them can finally ride out. What a wonderful day! The sun is shining, a young Arab foal is prancing around the paddock, the birds are singing and Lissi is delighted by the new stable building. Later, she will brush and feed her mare again, and cover her in her special present - a brand-new blanket.

name: Wash station with horse stall
description: After a long ride in the forest (dodging the falling pine cones), your horse deserves to be praised...and he definitely needs washed. Grab the curry comb and brush, it's up to you to freshen up the horse and style his mane. Your horse's coat is gonna shine by the time you're done! But first...your horse looks like he needs a funny foam beard from shampoo bubbles. Grooming time has never been so fun!

name: Stable with horses and accessories
description: Sarah has just returned from a ride with her Mustang mare, Maya. The new saddle fits perfectly! After rubbing her horse down, Sarah gives Maya fresh feed and straw - and maybe an apple or carrot for a treat. Then she'll have a quick snuggle with the little Mustang foal before heading home after a long day at the stables. This horse stable playset offers countless hours of limitless storytelling. The roof can be removed from the paddocks to convert the enclosure into a riding arena. Open the windows on the stable to let in the fresh air in while the horses exercise. Hay racks, drinking troughs and a tack rack add texture and realistic detail to the stable. The horse stable playset from schleich® FARM WORLD includes a stable, paddock, two mustang figurines (a mare and foal), and dozens of accessories like a horse blanket, bridle, and hay bale. schleich® farm toys for toddlers and kids introduce the endless possibilities of storytelling and imaginative play. From little donkey foal figurines to big barns filled with horses and cows, schleich® FARM WORLD has all the animal figures and farmer figures and sets you need to let your stories run wild! For even more action and adventures, our farm toys and figures play well with schleich® unicorn toys, dinosaur toys, wild animal figures and toy horses! schleich® playsets are built to last. All our toys meet the highest national and international safety standards.

name: Pinto foal
description: Pintos are often confused with Paint Horses, because they look very similar. However, Paint Horses are a specific breed, while pintos are defined by the appearance of their coat. This means that, in principle, their beautiful coat patterns can appear in any breed. The horses are then referred to as, for example, Arab pintos or Tinker pintos. There are even Pintaloosas - a mix of pinto and Appaloosa. As a rule of thumb: if you don't know the breed of a piebald horse, you can safely say it's a pinto. That's always correct.

name: Feeding set
description: Every horse has its favorite food. That is why it is recommended to have a special treat for each of these noble animals. Hay, straw, carrots and every now and then an apple, nearly every horse loves a treat like that. Happy feeding!

name: Saddle & bridle HORSE CLUB Hannah & Cayenne
description: The new saddle and bridle combos are especially colour-coordinated with one HORSE CLUB girl, but of course all other HORSE CLUB figures can ride with them too. The new saddle and bridle combos fit on almost all schleich® horses.

name: Saddle & bridle HORSE CLUB Sofia & Blossom
description: The new saddle and bridle combos are especially colour-coordinated with one HORSE CLUB girl, but of course all other HORSE CLUB figures can ride with them too. The new saddle and bridle combos fit on almost all schleich® horses.

name: Saddle & bridle HORSE CLUB Lisa & Storm
description: The new saddle and bridle combos are especially colour-coordinated with one HORSE CLUB girl, but of course all other HORSE CLUB figures can ride with them too. The new saddle and bridle combos fit on almost all schleich® horses.

name: Saddle & bridle HORSE CLUB Sarah & Mystery
description: The new saddle and bridle combos are especially colour-coordinated with one HORSE CLUB girl, but of course all other HORSE CLUB figures can ride with them too. The new saddle and bridle combos fit on almost all schleich® horses.

name: HORSE CLUB ride out
description: Join the four HORSE CLUB girls Hannah, Lisa, Sofia and Sarah with their horses Cayenne, Storm, Mystery and Blossom on their adventures around Lakeside. Where will their next ride take them?

name: Mr. Kramer's Riding Adventure
description: Are you ready for a road trip? With the Horse Adventures playset from schleich®, the destination is anywhere your imagination can travel - a riding tournament in the mountains, a horseback camping adventure, a trip to grandma's ranch or a ride on the moon. This equestrian road trip playset is part of the schleich® HORSE CLUB collection.

name: Hanoverian mare
description: In Germany, Hanoverians have been successfully bred and advanced according to the highest standard since 1735. The Hanoverian is a typical, modern-day sport horse in rectangular format (longer than high). The Hanoverian is characterised by an excellent motion, predestined for the great sport. It can be trained for both dressage and show jumping at the highest level and is considered teachable, attentive, balanced, but also spirited.

name: Caravan for secret club meetings
description: Be at the secret meeting place in an hour...this is gonna be big! A friend from the stables has a birthday coming up and the caravan is the perfect place to plan an epic surprise party. If only we had a secret place to hide the invitations. Can you think of somewhere we could hide them? Let's all head to the rooftop deck to plan snacks and party games! Best. Headquarters. EVER!

name: Appaloosa mare
description: Appaloosas' unique coat patterns will melt the heart of any horse fan. The beautiful spots and flecks have given rise to fantastical names such as leopard and snowflake. Appaloosas are often trained as western horses because they are reliable, fast and agile with strong nerves.

name: Miniature Shetland pony family
description: There's a new addition to the Miniature Shetland Pony family! The newborn foal's ungainliness makes everyone at the stables laugh. When the groom fills a feeding box with crunchy apples, the foal comes right over and curiously investigates it with its muzzle. Meanwhile, the stallion and the mare have started nosing at the bag of feed while nobody was looking. The groom shouldn't have let them out of her sight! The two Miniature Shetland Ponies are already energetically nibbling away to try and get to the feed inside the bags, which smells extremely tempting.

name: Paso Fino stallion horse show
description: The magnificent Paso Fino stallion is THE highlight at the big horse show in Lakeside. The onlookers are completely thrilled and in awe, because the stallion has a particularly smooth gait, which is even where the horse breed's name comes from. Paso Fino means precisely that: fine gait.

name: Trakehner mare riding tournament
description: Trakehner mares are gifted jumpers and dressage horses, and are often entered into shows. Because they have a sensitive temperament and quickly become distracted by noises, at shows they often wear a "fly veil" over their ears. This allows them to concentrate better on their jumps or dressage exercises.

name: Frisian stallion riding tournament
description: Frisian stallions are always an impressive sight at horse shows. They inherited their powerful build and even temper from coldbloods, while their elegant, energetic gait and proud bearing come from fiery Spanish horses. The white fly veil protects the Frisian stallion from loud noises, and the elegant white fetlock boots help prevent injuries.

name: Norwegian Fjord Horse Mare
description: The friendly Fjord Horse Mare looks so pretty today. Her dark brown hooves and muzzle stand out d from the rest of the coat, which is a striking yellow. Just like the Fjord Horse Gelding (sold separately), she has the typical dark stripe along her back, as well as the trademark of all Fjord Horses – a two-tone mane. She is especially proud of it, because her hair is trimmed into a nice stiff standing mane. It shows off her coloring perfectly. Doesn't she look adorable?

name: Horse Club Tori & Princess
description: Tori is happy: Finally, she's back in the stable with her Friesian Mare Princess. This is where she feels most at home. Today they want to go for a ride together. She spreads a saddle blanket on the mare's back and puts the saddle on. Last of all she puts the bridle on the horse. Of course, Tori is careful and always wears a helmet. Before she rides off, there is one thing left to do: She braids Princess's hair and ties bows in her black mane. It looks beautiful, doesn't it?

name: Hannah's Western Adventures
description: It’s finally here – Hannah’s western adventure can begin! She has decided to take part in barrel racing with her loyal Paint Horse Gelding. They have practiced hard for this tournament. The three barrels have already been set up on the paddock. The goal: To maneuver her horse round these barrels in a set sequence as quickly as possible. Are they fast enough to win? But before the tournament starts, Hannah warms up a little with her Paint Horse Gelding and has her jump a few obstacles. Of course, she wears a cowboy hat and sits in the western saddle. The two look great, don't they?

name: Lisa's Tournament Training
description: Nothing stands in the way of Lisa’s tournament training. She has already set up the two cavalettis on the riding paddock. Now she places the steep jump pole on top. She prepared the big obstacle yesterday and lovingly decorated it with stickers. The lunging whip is also ready. Now all that’s missing is her Appaloosa mare. She takes the mare out of the stable together with the little Appaloosa foal and it’s time to go. The pair focus their training on correct timing and jumping force to ensure that they take home a trophy at the next tournament. The big obstacle is still a challenge. But Lisa cleverly helps her mare with the lunging whip. The mare takes off and – hop! – overcomes the obstacle elegantly. The little Appaloosa foal watches closely. Is he already dreaming of his own tournament career?

name: Visit to the vet
description: Tori visits the vet with her horse Princess. She is worried. What’s wrong with your darling horse? The doctor, Mrs. Kramer, listens to the mare’s heart and breathing through the stethoscope. Hmm, I guess it’s best to be sure and take an X-ray too. To keep the horse still, she gives it a mild sedative first. Victoria is delighted: Princess has nothing serious. She just has to wear a bandage for a while. Mrs. Kramer also gives her a prescription with some medicine and Princess will soon be fit as a fiddle.

name: Collectible Foal Diamond
description: The adorable Diamond collectible foal is the star on the pasture. No wonder: No one else has such a beautiful shiny coat. It shines golden all over. When she and her friends are having fun in the meadow, it looks especially pretty. She raises her head curiously and pricks its ears ready for adventure. Maybe a playmate will come from that direction? The little foal with the golden coat has six friends, the others collectible foals. This cute herd is inseparable. Diamond is happiest when the foal herd is complete. Diamond gives her favourite person a poster of all of them together. It’s sure to look great in the children’s room! Who will be the first to collect all 4 foals?

name: Collectible Foal Darcy
description: The adorable collectible foal Darcy curiously raises his head and pricks up his ears ready for adventure. The temperament of the little rascal is immediately obvious. Expectantly, the foal raises her small, cream-coloured tail a little and stops in her tracks. There was a happy whinny coming from back there. She must check it out right away! The little foal with its grey-brown coat and cheeky appearance has six friends, the others collectible foals. This cute herd is inseparable. Darcy is happiest when the foal herd is complete. Darcy gives her favourite person a poster of all of them together. It’s sure to look great in the children’s room! Who will be the first to collect all 4 foals?

name: Collectible Foal Dipper
description: The adorable collectible foal Dipper curiously raises his head and pricks up his ears ready for adventure. He’s always there when there’s an adventure to be had. He is already stretching his neck with anticipation and approaches on his graceful legs. Perhaps he could polish off a crisp carrot? The little foal with its pretty white-brown coat and delicate pink nostrils has six friends, the other collectible foals. This cute herd is inseparable. Dipper is happiest when the foal herd is complete. Dipper gives her favourite person a poster of all of them together. It’s sure to look great in the children’s room! Who will be the first to collect all 4 foals?

name: Collectible Foal Delilah
description: The adorable collectible foal Delilah curiously raises her head and pricks up her ears ready for adventure. She looks over the meadow in anticipation. Maybe a playmate will come from that direction? The little foal with its cream coat and cute black nose has six friends, the others collectible foals. This cute herd is inseparable. Delilah is happiest when the foal herd is complete. Delilah gives her favourite person a poster of all of them together It’s sure to look great in the children’s room! Who will be the first to collect all 4 foals?

name: Sofia’s Horse Wash
description: Today Sofia wants to shower the American Saddlebred gelding. She leads him to the washing area. As a precaution, it is covered with a rubber mat to prevent the four-legged friend from slipping. Sofia presses the pump on the wooden barrel and the water flows out of the shower. With the practical swivelling arm, it carefully washes your horse from all sides. The shower water runs into the drip tray and from there back into the wooden barrel. This prevents the water cycle from breaking and the showering fun could go on forever. Marvellous!

name: Happy Horse Hospital
description: Oh dear! The handsome Andalusian stallion has injured his leg and is limping. At the "Healing Hooves" equine clinic, Mrs Kramer carefully examines the horse. She can take x-rays there and even perform minor operations. Fortunately, this is not necessary today. Nothing broken! She puts a supportive adhesive bandage on the horse and brings it into the cozy box to rest. Then she gives the stallion some crunchy apples. Everything will be fine!

name: Fjord Horse Mare - LOVE edition
description: The exclusive Valentine Edition 2026 with the enchanting Fjord Horse mare will capture the heart of every horse lover, making this limited edition the perfect Valentine's Day gift and a wonderful highlight for any collection.

name: English Thoroughbred Mare
description: The English Thoroughbred mare is slender and agile, yet muscular and strong. She outruns everyone in the race. Right now, however, she is taking it easy. She strides leisurely across the pasture to keep the handsome English thoroughbred stallion company. Where is it again?

name: English Thoroughbred Stallion
description: The handsome English thoroughbred stallion puts his ears up in a good mood and gallops across the pasture, his light brown mane fluttering. What is he so happy about? He has probably heard that he will soon be allowed to take part in a competition again. He particularly enjoys doing this. You can tell that he loves speed. His slender physique really comes into its own on the run. When will the time finally come?

name: Knabstrupper Gelding
description: The Knabstrupper gelding has a particularly pretty pattern with its snow-white coat and black spots. He always cuts a great figure when out riding. At the moment, however, he has something completely different in mind. He stretches his long neck forwards and pulls up his nose, sniffing. Maybe he wants to make eyes at the pretty Knabstrupper mare?

name: Knabstrupper Mare
description: The beautiful Knabstrupper mare with the eye-catching coat pattern attracts everyone's attention. The Knabstrupper gelding in particular has cast a spell over her. But she doesn't even realize that. She is completely absorbed in her powerful gallop across the pasture. She loves it when the wind plays in her thick mane and her tail flutters at full speed. Life is marvellous!

name: Lusitano Stallion
description: Does the impressive, jet-black Lusitano stallion know how beautiful he is? In any case, he clearly enjoys putting himself in the limelight in front of admiring glances. Now he is showing his best side again. He is about to stand on his hind legs and rears up majestically to his full height, shaking his flowing mane as he does so. A real show talent!

name: KWPN Gelding
description: The KWPN gelding is highly focussed. He is in the middle of a competition and wants to show what he can do in dressage. He has been rehearsing his piaffe for a long time, and now he finally performs it with pride. The white contrasting coat over the hooves emphasizes the stride particularly well. The gelding holds his neck in an elegant arch and shows off his beautiful braided hairstyle. He is sure to win the prize!

name: Mustang Mare
description: The spirited mustang mare not only looks wonderful with her white-brown piebald coat. She is also quite curious. Now she pricks up her ears attentively and her soft, black eyes look alertly into the distance. She must have heard something. Perhaps the neighing of the mustang stallion? She stamps her small, maneuverable hooves into the ground. She is ready to gallop off at any moment and throw herself into an adventure.

name: Mustang Stallion
description: This mustang stallion has a particularly fiery temperament, you can see that just by looking at him. It rises powerfully onto its strong hind legs and stretches its front legs into the air. He raises his slender head proudly. He loves freedom and adventure, there's no doubt about that. He loves to gallop across the prairie at a stretched gallop so that the dust swirls up under his hooves. Who's coming along?

name: Wild Life Ranger Adventure Station
description: Intrigue...danger...excitement...explore a landscape of heart-pounding adventure with the Ranger Adventure Station playset. Includes a treetop ranger station, a Ranger Steve figurine, a crocodile, a black panther, and dozens of realistic accesories. Suitable for thrill-seekers ages 3 and up.Schleich® wild animal figurines for toddlers and kids introduce the limitless possibilities of storytelling and imaginative play. From realistic ocean and jungle animal figurines to our popular playsets, Schleich® Wild Life lets your stories run wild. Authentic wild creature figurines toys prowl, swim, and soar with rich, accurate detail and texture, while our full-featured wild life playsets bring learning to life. Our wild animal toys love cross into new play worlds to create stories with Schleich® enchanted creature toys, farm animal figurines and dinosaur toys! Schleich® wild animal toys and playsets are built to last for generations. All our toys meet the highest national and international safety standards.

name: Wild Life Set - Wild animals
description: This is a colorful herd of animals in an exclusive set! The giraffe stretches its long neck to see what’s going on outside the box. Meanwhile, the mighty elephant starts to move with a slow, rocking motion. Who will explore the area with him? The curious Bonobo doesn't need asking twice. The ape gazes ahead, adventure in its eyes. The lion, on the other hand, still seems hesitant. He likes to observe first. And what about the crocodile? To join the other animals, he’d better not show off his long rows of teeth like that.

name: Outback Adventures
description: Imagine you're a young kangaroo, hopping among the scrub brush and playing hide-and-seek with friends. Off in the distance, you hear a cheerful whistling tune. It's Ranger Tom coming to visit! Hop up on your stump...can you see him hiking up the trail? G'day, Tom! Do you think the ranger and his friend the dingo will join your hide-and-seek game?

name: Penguin Family Fun
description: These penguin kids are pretty wild! The older penguin just can't get enough of the ice slide. Again and again it hurtles down the slippery ice chute. And splash - it lands in the water. It quickly swims back over to the ice floe with the slide. Wait what's that cracking sound? Oh, the little baby penguin is hatching from its egg! It wants to join in the penguin family fun right away. Look how cute it is, waddling awkwardly up the slope to get to the ice seesaw.

name: Wild Life Asia Starter Set
description: The four animal friends have come together from all corners of Asia. The female orangutan from Borneo dreamily scratches her reddish-brown fur with her movable arm. The giant panda with the pretty black and white markings on its fur and the funny spots around its eyes actually lives in China. But it has been spotted in children's rooms all over the world. The sweet tiger cub, with its cute striped fur, is at home all over Asia. And then there's the friendly baby elephant from Southeast Asia. It clearly feels at home together with its friends.

name: Wild Life Africa Starter Set
description: What an interesting bunch of animals! A zebra, a lion, an elephant and a chimpanzee – what on earth do they have in common? Of course! They all come from Africa! The lion is king of the animal world. No wonder, with his majestic mane and beautiful shining coat, loping majestically through the savannah. When the lion approaches, the zebra pricks up its ears and is ready to make a run for it. Normally, the two only spend time together at the watering hole. The little baby elephant isn't worried about the lion. It just wants to play with its friends. In real life the chimpanzee cub rarely gets to see a lion because it lives in the dense forests of Africa, far from the savannah. It spends its days swinging from tree to tree and performing daring antics.

name: Polar Playground
description: The two polar bears in the schleich® WILD LIFE Polar Playground have lots of fun on their ice floe: They hop and jump around wildly! Oh no!!! One of the polar bears ventured too close to the edge and fell in the water! Just as well that the friendly narwal is swimming around in the water nearby. It swims over right away to help the little polar bear back onto the ice floe!

name: Blue whale
description: The blue whale is the largest mammal on the planet. Its tongue alone weighs as much as an elephant, and when it opens its mouth, up to 90 tonnes of water can fit into it. It also has the biggest heart in the world, weighing 180 kg and making it about as large and heavy as a gorilla.

name: Mother wolf with pups
description: Wolf pups are blind and deaf at birth and therefore depend on their mother. Only after two weeks do they first open their eyes, and after about ten weeks they begin to explore the world around them. They like to play hide-and-seek, roll around in the grass and hunt small insects. As soon as they are two years old, they leave the pack to start one of their own.

name: Alpaca Set
description: The animals in the Alpaca Set from schleich® WILD LIFE come from the South American Andes, where they are bred for their soft, warm wool. They are hump-less camels with a calm and friendly temperament. The two young animals have different coats: One is white, the other brown. They are suckled by their mother for 6-8 months before starting to eat grass.

name: Gorilla Family
description: Leaning forward on her arms, the gorilla mom stands by her little ones and watches carefully to make sure they are safe. One of the baby gorillas is probably still a bit tired. It sits on the ground gazing around sleepily. Maybe it's time for a little nap. Its sibling is much more active: trying to stand, crawling on all fours through the jungle and even climbing onto its mother's back. There are so many different games to play with this lifelike lowland gorilla family. Maybe the cute babies will grow up to be big gorillas in your playroom someday?

name: Moose Family
description: The stately moose cow cares lovingly for her little calf. She suckles it several times a day. To keep her strength up, the mom needs to eat plenty of fresh grass. In search of a lush meadow, she stretches her long muzzle into the air and sniffs. When she gets the scent of some juicy grass, she strides off with big steps ahead through the forest with her calf following behind.

name: Drawstring Bag Llama
description: Our drawstring bag made from 100% high-quality cotton is ready for any adventure. The sturdy cotton cords are fastened without metal and make it particularly comfortable to carry. Thanks to its spaciousness, it offers enough room for many schleich® figurines.

name: Koala Mother and Baby
description: The Koala Mother and her baby from schleich® WILD LIFE love a good breakfast of eucalyptus together. Eu-ca-lyp-tus...say that 10 times fast without getting tongue-tied! After breakfast, the little joey hangs on tight onto his momma's back as she carries him up into the trees.

name: Killer Whale
description: The orca is a large, toothed whale that belongs to the dolphin family. Its black and white colouring makes its unmistakable. Animals swimming under it don't recognise it, because the surface of the water is just as bright as its belly. And from above, you can hardly tell its apart from the sea floor thanks to its black back.

name: African elephant, female
description: Elephants are known as being thick skinned. However, their skin is so sensitive, they can even get sunburnt. That is why elephants often throw several layers of mud on themselves. This pleasant "cooling pack" also combats the scorching heat. While elephants cannot hear any better than other animals with their huge ears, they help them to cope with the African heat. When they fan with their ears, the blood cools down and provides them with refreshment.

name: African elephant, male
description: Elephant bulls are considerably bigger than elephant cows. Reaching heights of almost four metres, they are the second largest land animals after giraffes. They also weigh five to six tonnes, which makes them the heaviest of all land animals. African elephant bulls usually leave their herd when they are about twelve years old. By then they are grown-up enough to find their own food and defend themselves, so they move through the country with other bulls or as a loner - in search of another herd.

name: Limited-Edition Frosty Christmas Reindeer
description: Ho, ho, ho! The Limited-Edition Frosty Christmas Reindeer is all dressed up and looking forward to the holiday season. Finally it can go on the road again with Santa Claus and transport all the children's gifts in the sleigh. Every year it's a truly special experience. The reindeer stretches its neck and pricks up its ears: Didn't it hear something? A ringing of bells, perhaps? That must be Santa Claus already preparing the sleigh before the great ride! It should probably go and check.

name: Asian elephant, female (bundle)
description: The trunk of an elephant is as versatile as a Swiss army knife. Not only can the elephant smell really well with it, it uses it to suck in water, steady itself, lift trees up high, or spray itself with cool mud. The elephant eats by wrapping its trunk around leaves and grasses, whereby it plucks them from the tree and feeds them into its mouth. A grown animal devours up to 150 kg of green foliage a day. Elephants can even pronounce human-like words with their trunks. To do this, they put their trunks in the mouth and place it on the tongue.

name: Giant Panda
description: With its striking black and white fur, and black circles around its eyes, the giant panda looks just like a cuddly toy. But today it doesn't have time to cuddle. The bear, which can weigh up to 160 kilos, has to make sure it gets enough food. Because it eats almost nothing but bamboo, and the plant is not particularly nutritious, it has to keep eating for up to 16 hours a day! No wonder it has no time to play; it has to digest up to 40 kilos of bamboo! And that takes time.

name: Grizzly bear (circular)
description: Grizzlies are tall, strong animals - they stand up to 2.4 metres high and weigh roundabout 450 kilograms. They have sort of a hump on their back and strong hind limbs. Grizzly bears are able to run up to 55 kilometres per hour. They are loners and frequently behave aggressively. Sometimes they gather at waters to catch salmon. Since they are omnivores, these bears feed on fish, plants and large mammals like moose, deer and black bears. They are also known to steal food from wolves or humans.

name: Polar bear (circular)
description: The polar bear's habitat is the Arctic. In winter, the temperature there can drop to -50° Celsius. Although its fur coat appears white, its hair is transparent and hollow on the inside. The air inside these hairs is warmed by the sun, so that polar bears do not freeze even at icy temperatures.

name: Gorilla, male
description: Male gorillas often beat their fists on their chests. They do so to show who is the strongest in the group. However, it can also be a kind of greeting. Gorillas don't just walk on the palms of their hands, but also on their knuckles. For this reason, they're also known as knuckle-walkers.

name: Black Lion, roaring
description: Stop, stop whatever you're doing right now! And take a look. The Black Friday Lion from schleich® WILD LIFE is a real eye-catcher. And with his majestic mane and unique color, he just stands out. Which actually makes him quite nervous. He prefers to prance in private through the savannah or your playroom. And pretend not to see the stares – because that's not cool. At least that's what he thinks, even if he doesn't really know what that actually means. Maybe you can explain it to him?

name: Giraffe, female
description: Despite the camouflage spots in their fur, giraffes do not always remain undetected by predators. But they know to defend themselves and can kick really hard with their front hooves. They mainly do this if they want to protect their young, because the adult animals are normally rarely attacked. Anyone who has the opportunity to look at giraffes from close up can easily distinguish the females from the males. They are somewhat smaller and have thin horns with a small tuft of fur on top. Giraffe bulls, in contrast, have thick horns with no fur on top.

name: Crocodile
description: The saltwater crocodile is the most dangerous of all crocodiles. It holds the record for the largest crocodile: It can weigh up to a ton, as much as a small car. While most crocodiles live in rivers and lakes, the saltwater crocodile is the only one that can deal with both fresh water and saltwater of the seas. In addition to the saltwater crocodile, there are many other kinds, a total of 25. These include, for example, the Nile crocodile, the armoured crocodile, alligators and caimans.

name: Zebra female
description: Zebras live in the African savannah and belong to the horse family. Each zebra has a unique fur coat pattern consisting of black and white stripes. Even its mane is striped. The pattern provides camouflage, confuses predators, protects against mosquitoes, makes the heat more bearable, and helps the animals to recognise each other.

name: Manatee
description: The Manatee from schleich® WILD LIFE is a mammal that lives in tropical waters and feeds on seagrass, algae and other aquatic plants. Its closest relative is an elephant. Its front legs have evolved into fins over millions of years, while its hind legs have completely disappeared. The manatee's tail is shaped like a paddle, whereas a dugong, which looks very similar, has a tail fluke that looks like a dolphin.

name: Alligator
description: The alligator is a dangerous predator with exceptionally strong jaw muscles. Only the white shark can bite harder than him. The interesting thing: Only the muscles that close the jaws are extremely strong. The muscles that open the snout are so weak that they can be closed with simple tape. Compared to the saltwater crocodile, alligators are smaller and much wider. They live only in rivers and lakes in the south-eastern part of the United States and China.

name: Tiger, white
description: White tigers are breathtakingly beautiful. They have a snow-white fur and strong, bright blue eyes. They differ from normal tigers because fur, skin and eyes cannot form red and yellow pigments. However, they are not albinos, for those always have red eyes. White tigers usually live in zoos because in the wild they would have little chance of survival. Because their fur is missing the camouflage, prey would discover them quickly and run away early enough.

name: Giraffe, male
description: Giraffe bulls are so large, they can put their head through the window of the second floor of a building and say "hello". Their tongue is up to 50 centimetres long, is desensitised to prickly leaves, and has an unusual colour: it is dark blue. This colour helps protect it from sunburn when giraffes use it to wrap around and pluck leaves. Even animals as large as giraffes need to sleep between meals. When there is no threat of danger, they lie down for a nap, but in the wilderness they mainly sleep standing up.

name: Moose bull
description: The elk is Sweden's national animal and is known for its impressive antlers. However, only male elk have them. Elk bulls start growing the antlers at the age of two. They can reach a weight of up to 20 kg. The bulls shed them each year and grow a new set.

name: Great white shark
description: The great white shark is the largest predatory fish on the planet. The way its teeth work is somewhat reminiscent of a revolver and they consist of several rows. When one of its sharp, triangular teeth breaks out of the front row, it is quickly replaced by another from the row behind it. New replacement teeth are constantly growing.

name: Hippopotamus
description: Although hippopotamuses cannot swim particularly well, they spend nearly all day in the water. They cannot simply drift along, because they're just too heavy. If they wish to cross a river, they allow themselves to sink to the bottom and walk along the river bed to the other shore.

name: Dromedary
description: Dromedaries live in the deserts of Africa and Asia. They eat mostly leaves, dry grass and thorny plants. The hump of the mammal isn't used to store water; it stores fat. If they can't find any food in the desert they can live off it for weeks. The dromedary from schleich® WILD LIFE has eaten well recently and has an upright, well-filled hump.

name: Mandrill
description: What if a miniature Mandrill figurine were more than a toy? What if a monkey could turn a slowly ticking clock into the sounds of a jungle at night? What if a Mandrill could speak a secret language only a child can understand? Some monkeys are good at swinging from vines...and some, like this one, help the littlest storytellers let their imaginations run wild. schleich® wild animal figurines for toddlers and kids introduce the limitless possibilities of storytelling and imaginative play. From realistic ocean and jungle animal figurines to our popular playsets, schleich® WILD LIFE lets your stories run wild. Authentic wild creature figurines toys prowl, swim, and soar with rich, accurate detail and texture, while our full-featured wild life playsets bring learning to life. Our wild animal toys love cross into new play worlds to create stories with schleich® enchanted creature toys, farm animal figurines and dinosaur toys! schleich® wild animal toys and playsets are built to last for generations. All our toys meet the highest national and international safety standards.

name: African Buffalo
description: The mighty Cape Buffalo raises its head and sniffs the air. With its black muzzle, it can sniff out exactly whether strangers are approaching the herd. The buffalo finds it hard to decide: can they keep on grazing or is an enemy closing in? If a strange buffalo tries to approach the herd, it doesn't miss a beat. At a gallop, he charges the uninvited guest, stopping just short of the stranger and lowers its big head. It snorts angrily, pawing at the ground. Its huge horns look so impressive that most intruders will trot off right away. All the better!

name: Reindeer
description: Reindeer are a deer species that live in large herds in the colder regions of North America, northern Europe and northern Asia. In nature they have the same stick-like antlers as the one from schleich® WILD LIFE. They are the only deer species where the female also has antlers. Another special feature is their hooves. They are very wide and spread as they walk. This creates a characteristic creaking or cracking sound.

name: Arctic Wolf
description: The Arctic wolf pricks up its pointed ears and watches closely with its beautiful, amber-colored eyes to see what’s going on. Is the pack getting ready to leave? That’s fine, because it's hungry. Time to go hunting. The wolves always go hunting in the pack. With their snowy-white fur, they are perfectly camouflaged in the Arctic. And the cute Arctic wolf cub can come along too. Let’s go!

name: Chameleon
description: The chameleon is perfectly prepared for life in trees with its clawed feet and strong curly tail. So it can cling to branches with its body without being blown down by the wind. Of course it can also camouflage itself by adapting the color of its scaly skin to the tree or rock it happens to be sitting on. But sometimes it changes color when it wants to attract attention. Can your chameleon grab your attention like that?

name: Chimpanzee
description: The chimpanzee is calmly standing by, watching its large family. Someone is always in the mood for a game or a nimble climb on one of the jungle giants. The chimpanzee is happy. It’s watching its young one take its first steps. That kid is sure to become a great climber one day! Maybe it will be a gymnast too? That would be a plus for the whole family!

name: Lion, roaring
description: The larger the mane of a lion, the more frightening it is for intruders of its territory, which it defends. Lions are the only ones who hunt together in prides - although this task is usually left to the lionesses. Since they are not very fast, unlike the cheetah, they use their strength as a pride. They prowl from different directions, circle their prey and use the element of surprise. That way they can kill large ungulates such as wildebeest and zebras and even buffalos and weak elephants.

name: Lion
description: In order to become the leader of the pack, lions must win a duel. The larger their mane, the more respect they are shown by their rival. But not only does it look breath-taking - it also protects the animals from serious injuries on their heads and necks.

name: Kangaroo
description: These marsupials have a kind of rubber band in their hind legs. It propels them upwards with each jump. The faster a kangaroo bounces, the farther the animal is "catapulted" forwards, and the less energy it consumes.

name: Jaguar
description: Jaguars have the strongest bite of any big cat: twice as strong as that of a lion. With it, jaguars can even crack open tortoise shells without any effort. It's easy to tell jaguars apart from leopards, since they have spots in the middle of the flower-shaped markings on their coats.

name: Black panther
description: Jaguars and leopards with black coats are called black panthers. Despite their black coats, they still have the characteristic flower-shaped markings. Since these markings are as dark as their coat, you can only see them when the light is right.

name: White-tailed buck
description: White-tailed bucks are very shy animals. When they get scared, they wave their tails back and forth. If there is indeed a predator nearby, the deer stretch their tails upwards. The white fur that gives them their name becomes clearly visible. The biggest enemies of the white-tailed deer are wolves, coyotes, pumas, bears, alligators and jaguars.

name: Wolf
description: It is said that wolves howl at the moon, but their sounds actually serve to communicate with each other. By howling, they want to draw the attention of the other pack members to something important, call them to hunt together, or try to drive away wolves that don't belong to the pack. Their cries can be heard for many kilometres.

name: Lioness
description: With lions, it is almost exclusively the females that go hunting. Many of their prey are faster than they are, so these predators first creep up to a few meters from different sides and then sprint towards their prey. Even though the lionesses usually do the hunting, the males are always the first ones to eat.

name: Snow Leopard
description: The snow leopard is a large cat that lives in the high rocky mountains of Central Asia. It feeds mainly on sheep, ibex and goats. Thick fur protects it against freezing cold temperatures but its beauty means that many animals fall prey to poachers. That's why the snow leopard is an endangered species. A special feature is its tail which, like the snow leopard from schleich® WILD LIFE, is nearly as long as its body.

name: Proboscis Monkey
description: The Proboscis Monkey from schleich® WILD LIFE is a primate that lives in rainforests and mangrove forests on the Asian island of Borneo. The males have a strikingly large nose that can be up to 10 cm long. But they don't have a strong sense of smell. Researchers believe that it is used to impress females. Proboscis Monkeys are day-active mammals who live in small groups and feed on leaves and fruits.

name: Tapir
description: Tapirs eat as much as 75 pounds of fruit, berries, and leaves a day, so be sure to stock your refrigerator with lots of fresh produce. This one has a HUUUUUUGE appetite! In fact, this tapir likes foraging in the pantry after you go to sleep, and might even eat the veggies off your dinner plate if you don't pay attention. It's a true story...on of a million gazillion true stories our little tapir will star in once you bring it home. schleich® wild animal figurines for toddlers and kids introduce the limitless possibilities of storytelling and imaginative play. name: Iguana
description: WWUUAAAARRR! Although the Iguana from schleich® WILD LIFE is smaller than its reptile relatives, it is still a tough little lizard. Flaky skin protects it from sharp teeth and UV light. So it doesn't need to be afraid of little enemies or sunscreen. Our little crawler also has a tail that can be almost twice as long as the rest of its body. Perfect for defense or drawing pictures in the sand. With its magnificent head, our brave Iguana not only nods in time with the beat at concerts, but also communicates with other iguanas. This can sometimes lead to dance-talk misunderstandings.

name: Ibex
description: Skillfully, the Ibex climbs up and down among the high mountain rocks. Setting its dainty hooves with caution, with an eye on the trail, the Ibex can move effortlessly through the difficult terrain of the Alps. More confidently than any other mammal here. It can easily scramble to heights of 3,500 meters! But when it gets hungry, it usually descends again from those lofty heights, because the juicy alpine meadows with the tasty herbs are usually lower down.

name: American Black Bear
description: The American Black Bear wanders through the woods at a leisurely pace. Where is it going? It's probably on the look-out for food as usual. Thanks to its stately size it's almost always hungry. Its favorite hobby is fishing. There it is, standing right in the middle of the river, skillfully catching a salmon in its mighty paws. If it gets really lucky, it might even find some honey in a tree hollow. Thanks to its thick bristly coat, it's almost impossible for the bees to get through to sting it.

name: Japanese Macaque
description: The Japanese Macaque looks so friendly. It almost looks as if it is smiling, tilting its bushy head with the small reddish face: "Do you have a treat for me?" Who could resist! Like all monkeys, this macaque loves fresh fruit. If it's sweet, like bananas, it loves it all the more. The Japanese Macaque is curious and sociable and likes to make friends. Once it has got to know you, it's sure to let you cuddle its thick furry back.

name: Giant panda, male
description: Pandas have a striking black and white coat that makes it easier for other pandas to spot them in dense woodland. Scientists are sure this must be the explanation for it, since pandas don't need to worry about camouflaging themselves. They are so large and heavy that they have no natural enemies.

name: Thomson Gazelle
description: Is that a rustling in the bushes? The pretty Thompson gazelle carefully observes its surroundings. There might be a cheetah hiding somewhere. The gazelle isn't interested in ending up as that cat's breakfast! Unfortunately, its long, twisted horns are of little use against the cheetah. The best mode of action is to run away, dodging left and right like a pro. Or to stays put, where the grass is low and it has a good overview. Actually, the living room carpet is a pretty safe bet, too!

name: Bonobo Female (circular)
description: The female Bonobo looks very much like a Chimpanzee. No wonder: both come from the same ape family. But experts can recognize bonobos immediately by their light lips and distinctive middle parting. A pretty cool hairstyle for the female Bonobo, don't you think?

name: Koala (circular)
description: Koalas are quite the sleepy heads. They spend about 14 hours each day taking extensive naps. The rest of the time, they are busy eating eucalyptus leaves. They consume about a kilogram of them every day. They are very picky and will only go for 12 of the about 300 eucalyptus species.

name: Beaver (circular)
description: Like most beavers, this one built his lodge out of branches he chomped on his own...but now he's eager to find a place in your home. He's a hard worker, well-behaved, and comes with an excellent dental plan. schleich® wild animal figurines for toddlers and kids introduce the limitless possibilities of storytelling and imaginative play.
